# NHSN Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Resource Center

The dQM Resource Center is for a wide audience of hospital staff who implement NHSN digital quality measures. Each page includes resources for both clinical and IT implementers that will help establish and maintain accurate reporting to NHSN’s dQMs.

[About FHIR

Learn how NHSN FHIR dQMs are reported via NHSNLink, NHSN’s FHIR application.](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html)

[FHIR Ready?

A guide for facility stakeholders to prepare for FHIR submissions.](/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/fhir-ready.html)

[FAQs

Common questions from FHIR implementers](/nhsn/fhirportal/faqs.html)

[NHSN dQM Implementation Support

NHSN HAI Implementation Guides, IDMs and toolkits.](/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/ach-dQMs.html#anchor_10162)

[Acute Care Hospital dQMs

Measure information, Specifications, Measure Resources, and Technical Release notes](/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/ach-dQMs.html)

# About FHIR and NHSNLink

![health care image for dqm](/nhsn/fhirportal/img/health-care-image-for-dqm-large.jpg?_=13491 "Making the Connection")

Making the Connection

The National Healthcare Safety Network’s (NHSN) effort to modernize hospital surveillance through Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) using Fast Healthcare Interoperability Reporting (FHIR) will transform hospital reporting. This page provides information for implementers and hospital staff on how FHIR will utilize NHSNLink to exchange hospital data between facilities and NHSN.

**On This Page**

* [About FHIR](#anchor_24110)
* [About NHSN FHIR dQMs](#anchor_01227)
* [Is Your Facility FHIR Ready?](#anchor_14754)

* [About NHSNLink](#anchor_96959)
* [Data Security and Privacy](#anchor_72781)

## About FHIR

Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources® ([FHIR](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/)) is a Health Level Seven International® (HL7) standard for exchanging healthcare information electronically between information systems. FHIR has several advantages over other methods of reporting, as shown in the figure below.

Difference between Manual, Semi-Automated, and Digital Quality Measures
title: About FHIR and NHSNLink

ig: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
igTitle: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
text:
Resource Center Page: About FHIR and NHSNLink
Page key: about
URL: https://www.cdc.gov/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html
Category: onboarding
Audience: ehr-vendor-team, laboratory-data-team, medication-data-team

nically between information systems. FHIR has several advantages over other methods of reporting, as shown in the figure below.

Difference between Manual, Semi-Automated, and Digital Quality Measures

| **With Manual or Semi-Automated Measures** | With Digital Quality Measures |
| --- | --- |
| Data standards are specific to the measure and the organization to which they are reported | Data are represented using nationally recognized standards across the EHR vendors, facilities, and agencies |
| Data are pushed (NHSN waits for the facility to transmit data) | Data can be pulled, making real-time surveillance feasible |
| Data are often aggregated, *facility*-level risk adjustment is typical | Data are at the patient level, *patient*-level risk adjustment is possible |
| Measures are pre-determined before transmission | Measures can be adapted after data transmission |

About NHSN FHIR dQMs

Healthcare facilities can use FHIR application programming interfaces (APIs) to report [digital quality measures](https://ecqi.healthit.gov/dqm) (dQMs) to NHSN. FHIR dQMs enable automated, patient-level data reporting, which minimizes long product cycles and provides access to crucial healthcare data. FHIR dQMs can be implemented in various information systems and healthcare settings, and across different electronic health record (EHR) vendors.

NHSN Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) Under Development

Several concepts are under exploration and development by NHSN as dQMs. Where possible, NHSN will strive to align with future CMS dQM initiatives.

Table 1: NHSN Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) Under Development
title: About FHIR and NHSNLink

cepts are under exploration and development by NHSN as dQMs. Where possible, NHSN will strive to align with future CMS dQM initiatives.

Table 1: NHSN Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) Under Development

| **Measure** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Healthcare facility-onset, antibiotic-treated *C. difficile* infection (CDI) | Rate of hospital-onset CDI events among hospitalized patients as measured by a combination of laboratory findings and medication treatments |
| Hospital-onset bacteremia and fungemia (HOB) | Rate of HOB events among hospitalized patients as measured by microbiology findings (irrespective of organism or device) |
| Healthcare-associated venous thromboembolism (VTE) | Feasibility exploration of using FHIR API to automatically extract and generate VTE prophylaxis and VTE outcome rates |
| Inpatient medication-related hypoglycemia | Rate of hypoglycemia events among hospitalized patients treated with diabetes medications |
| Inpatient hyperglycemia | Rate of hyperglycemia events among hospitalized patients with diabetes |
| Respiratory Pathogen Surveillance | Rate of confirmed positive cases/events of influenza, respiratory syncytial virus, or COVID-19 among hospitalized patients |
| Sepsis | Feasibility exploration of using FHIR API to automatically extract and generate sepsis-related data elements from an EHR |
| Late Onset Sepsis / Meningitis (LOS/MEN) | Rate of LOS and MEN events among selected hospitalized infants who meet event criteria as per the NHSN LOS/MEN protocol based on a combination of laboratory findings and medication treatments |

Clinical Query Language (CQL)

Clinical Query Language (CQL)

The NHSN FHIR dQM defines NHSN measure concepts using HL7 [clinical query language](http://cql.hl7.org/history.html) (CQL). CQL is machine-readable and structured on the FHIR data model. The CDC NHSN dQM definition aligns with the Centers for Medicaid and Medicare Services dQM definition in the [CMS Digital Quality Measurement Strategic Roadmap](https://ecqi.healthit.gov/sites/default/files/CMSdQMStrategicRoadmap_032822.pdf).

The NHSN FHIR dQM CQL defines the following:

* Patients of interest eligible for the NHSN dQM, as defined by the NHSN protocol
* Data requirements and value sets for identifying the patients of interest that NHSNLink will pull (see [About NHSNLink](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html#anchor_96959))
* Patient-level data required for the patients of interest as defined by the dQM, including the numerator and denominator as defined by the NHSN protocol
* Additional patient-level data for stratification, risk adjustment, identifying social determinants of health, and patient matching

See the NHSN dQM Implementation Guide **{coming soon}** for additional information.

## Is Your Facility FHIR Ready?

If you are a healthcare facility interested in reporting data to NHSN via FHIR, start by talking to your IT staff to determine if your facility is FHIR-ready:

![checkmark](/nhsn/images/checkmark.png?_=20163) Are your FHIR R4 APIs deployed to your production environment?

## About NHSNLink

![nhsnlink logo](/nhsn/images/nhsnlink.png?_=43570)

NHSNLink is CDC’s FHIR application for reporting digital quality measures to NHSN, published under the Apache 2.0 open-source license.

How NHSNLink Works
title: About FHIR and NHSNLink

ig: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
igTitle: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
text:
Resource Center Page: About FHIR and NHSNLink
Page key: about
URL: https://www.cdc.gov/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html
Category: onboarding
Audience: ehr-vendor-team, laboratory-data-team, medication-data-team

ink logo](/nhsn/images/nhsnlink.png?_=43570)

NHSNLink is CDC’s FHIR application for reporting digital quality measures to NHSN, published under the Apache 2.0 open-source license.

How NHSNLink Works

Housed behind the CDC firewall, NHSNLink has a multi-component architecture that uses an extensible and configurable query engine to connect NHSN to a healthcare facility’s EHR securely via the FHIR API, extract data, and submit it to the NHSN’s back-end analytic environment. NHSNLink is accessible to any clinical data application with a FHIR Release 4 (or later) API, irrespective of EHR vendor or third-party application. NHSNLink queries the EHR for the patient census, extracting the data required to calculate the measure for each reporting period. Once securely connected to the facility FHIR API, NHSNLink uses HL7 FHIR quality measure specifications to create a MeasureReport Bundle composed of a patient list and respective FHIR resources for those patients. The MeasureReport bundle is then sent to the NHSN application for data quality processes and measure calculation, risk adjustment, stratification, and other operations. The measure results are provided back to the healthcare facility via the NHSN web-based public interface, allowing NHSN users to further analyze and interpret their surveillance data to identify targets for quality improvement and patient safety.

![How NHSNLink interacts with NHSN and facility environment.](/nhsn/fhirportal/img/nhsnlink-interactions.png?_=88990 "NHSNLink Interactions")

\*Signals facility readiness to report digital quality measure and that facility has signed NHSN data-use agreements for secure data-sharing.
title: About FHIR and NHSNLink

Resource Center Page: About FHIR and NHSNLink

hsnlink-interactions.png?_=88990 "NHSNLink Interactions")

\*Signals facility readiness to report digital quality measure and that facility has signed NHSN data-use agreements for secure data-sharing.

API = application programing interface; dQM = digital quality measure; FHIR = Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources; SAMS = Secure Access Management Services.

1. Confirm facility enrollment in digital measure reporting plan; request and receive NHSN FHIR dQM
2. Request and receive Patients of Interest and query data defined by dQM
3. Evaluate and filter data as defined by dQM
4. Submit MeasureReport bundle for patients meeting dQM definition
5. NHSN ingests and analyzes MeasureReport bundles and makes reports available via secure NHSN user interface

Data Security and Privacy

Data within NHSNLink (as with all NHSN systems) are protected by administrative, technical, and physical security controls that safeguard the confidentiality, integrity, and privacy of personal information according to industry-standard policies and federal laws as described [here [PDF – 390 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/NHSNLink_Architecture_and_Information_508c.pdf). Because NHSNLink submits the normalized data to NHSN’s digital reporting tables, it must adhere to certain technical specifications and security protocols. NHSNLink maintains an audit trail of its operations and, where required, includes report-submission metadata to allow downstream analytics.

FHIR Ready | NHSN | CDC

# Preparing Your Facility for Reporting FHIR Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) to NHSN

###### **AT A GLANCE**

* **Prepare Stakeholders**: Engage key personnel at your facility for the transition to reporting digital quality measures (dQMs) using Healthcare Level Seven International (HL7®) Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources® (FHIR®).

* **Appoint a Coordinator**: Appoint a staff member to serve as the NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator to oversee reporting tasks.

* **Task Guide**: Use this page as a resource to prepare for reporting dQMs to NHSN and for compliance tasks.

![Medical Test](/nhsn/fhirportal/img/image.jpg?_=58925)

**On This Page**

* [Preparing to Report dQMs to NHSN](#anchor_18529)
* [Early Adopters](#anchor_1778518748)

* [NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator](#anchor_86919)
* [Additional Information](#anchor_32762)

### Preparing to Report dQMs to NHSN

As part of the Digital Measure Reporting Plan, the facility will authorize [NHSNLink](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html#anchor_96959) to retrieve data from the designated health system-level FHIR application programming interfaces (APIs) to support transmission and calculation of [digital quality measures (dQM](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html)s) to NHSN. This process follows established data governance protocols and ensures alignment with privacy and security standards.
title: Preparing Your Facility for Reporting FHIR Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) to NHSN

ig: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
igTitle: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
text:
Resource Center Page: Preparing Your Facility for Reporting FHIR Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) to NHSN
Page key: fhir-ready
URL: https://www.cdc.gov/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/fhir-ready.html
Category: onboarding
Audience: implementation-coordinator, facility-administrator, ehr-vendor-team, laboratory-data-team, medication-data-team, executive-informatics

ulation of [digital quality measures (dQM](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html)s) to NHSN. This process follows established data governance protocols and ensures alignment with privacy and security standards.

To submit FHIR digital quality measures (dQMs) to NHSN via NHSNLink, facilities must ensure their data comply with NHSN’s requirements as specified in the [CDC NHSN dQM Content Package IG](/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/ig/). Facilities should identify personnel who will serve as the champions or liaisons throughout preparation and implementation. These staff ensure the facility completes the necessary tasks before initial connection with NHSNLink and support the facility’s FHIR connection throughout the reporting process. NHSN will update this list of tasks periodically as we prepare for fully automated data exchange with healthcare facilities.

### Early Adopters

NHSN will soon expand voluntary submission of FHIR-based digital quality measure (dQM) data beyond pilot facilities participating in the [NHSN CoLab](/nhsn/nhsncolab/index.html). Early Adopter facilities will be invited to voluntarily submit dQM data to NHSN to help test data submission functionality and analytic reports within the application, and to identify training and support needs prior to broader national expansion.

Facilities interested in becoming **Early Adopters** are encouraged to check their FHIR readiness in advance.

**Check Your FHIR Readiness**

Facilities can check their FHIR R4 API conformance to **US Core v6.1.0**, which serves as the baseline for the NHSN FHIR Implementation Guide.

**Recommended validation tool:** Use the [**Inferno US Core Test Kit**](https://inferno.healthit.gov/test-kits/us-core/) and run the test suite **“US Core Server v6.1.0.”**

**How to get started:**
title: Preparing Your Facility for Reporting FHIR Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) to NHSN

ig: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
igTitle: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
text:
Resource Center Page: Preparing Your Facility for Reporting FHIR Digital Quality Measures (dQMs) to NHSN
Page key: fhir-ready
URL: https://www.cdc.gov/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/fhir-ready.html

Audience: implementation-coordinator, facility-administrator, ehr-vendor-team, laboratory-data-team, medication-data-team, executive-informatics

.

**Recommended validation tool:** Use the [**Inferno US Core Test Kit**](https://inferno.healthit.gov/test-kits/us-core/) and run the test suite **“US Core Server v6.1.0.”**

**How to get started:**

1. Go to the Inferno **US Core Test Kit**
2. Select Test Suite: “**US Core Server v6.1.0.**”
3. Execute the tests against your production-like FHIR R4 endpoint and resolve any failures before starting NHSN dQM reporting.

**Important privacy note:** Inferno on HealthIT.gov is a public demonstration environment provided by ONC and is **not approved for sensitive data/PHI**; use synthetic test data and/or run Inferno locally for internal testing.

More information about becoming an early adopter for NHSN dQMs will be released later in 2026.

###

#### NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator

The NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator is crucial for the success of the facility’s health system in this effort. This individual will:

* Coordinate with the NHSN Facility Administrator for submission of FHIR dQMs to NHSN.
* Serve as an educator for key personnel at the facility involved with reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN
* Ensure the IT team is aware of and has implemented [Key Action Items for Reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN [PDF – 85 MB]](/nhsn/pdfs/FHIR-Optimizing-Reporting.pdf).
* Share feedback with NHSN about the facility’s experience in implementing reporting of FHIR dQMs to NHSN.

**NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator**

#### Staff and Facility Roles

**NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator**

* Become familiar with information available in the [NHSN FHIR Portal](/nhsn/fhirportal/index.html) to support connectivity between your facility’s electronic health data and NHSNLink
* Invite facility stakeholders to view previously recorded [informational sessions](/nhsn/training/annualtraining/2024-nhsn-training.html) provided by NHSN about FHIR dQM reporting
* Connect with facility stakeholders on their roles and responsibilities in pre-work for NHSN FHIR dQM implementation

#### Examples of Responsibilities

* Become familiar with information available in the [NHSN FHIR Portal](/nhsn/fhirportal/index.html) to support connectivity between your facility’s electronic health data and NHSNLink
* Invite facility stakeholders to view previously recorded [informational sessions](/nhsn/training/annualtraining/2024-nhsn-training.html) provided by NHSN about FHIR dQM reporting
* Connect with facility stakeholders on their roles and responsibilities in pre-work for NHSN FHIR dQM implementation

**Executive Suite–Informatics (e.g** **Chief Health Information Officer, Chief Medical Information Officer, Chief Data Officer, Chief Information Officer)**

#### Staff and Facility Roles

**Executive Suite–Informatics (e.g** **Chief Health Information Officer, Chief Medical Information Officer, Chief Data Officer, Chief Information Officer)**

* Provide approval for preparation to report FHIR dQMs to NHSN
* Designate individuals and teams that will have oversight of clinical activities related to reporting FHIR dQMs to [NHSN dQMs Under Development](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html)

**NHSN Facility Administrator**

#### Staff and Facility Roles

**NHSN Facility Administrator**

* Review and sign the monthly Digital Reporting Plan
* Work with IT to ensure the facility’s patient-care locations are all correctly mapped according to NHSN [Healthcare Facility Patient Care Location codes](/nhsn/cdaportal/terminology/codesystem/hsloc.html) (HSLOC)
* Add new stakeholders who have been identified as critical to FHIR dQM implementation as additional users for the facility in NHSN

**IT – EHR Vendor Team**

#### Staff and Facility Roles

**IT – EHR Vendor Team**

* Review the [National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measure (dQM) Reporting Implementation Guide](https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/nhsn-dqm/index.html) and the [CDC NHSN Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG](/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/ig/)
* Review the requested FHIR APIs ([QI Core 6 [PDF – 255 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/EHR_QICore6.pdf), [US Core 3 [PDF – 256 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/EHR_USCore3.pdf)) with the EHR vendor for availability and accessibility
* Confirm requested FHIR R4 APIs are in Production and conform to [US Core v6.1.0](https://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/STU6.1/)
* Review [Key Action Items for Reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN [PDF – 86 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/FHIR-Optimizing-Reporting.pdf)
* Review [Overview of Requested FHIR Resources and Standards Adherence for Reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN [XLS – 26 KB]](/nhsn/xls/dQM-Reporting-Required-FHIR-Data.xlsx)

Review [Overview of Requested FHIR Resources and Standards Adherence for Reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN [XLS – 26 KB]](/nhsn/xls/dQM-Reporting-Required-FHIR-Data.xlsx)

#### Examples of Responsibilities

* Review the [National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measure (dQM) Reporting Implementation Guide](https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/nhsn-dqm/index.html) and the [CDC NHSN Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG](/nhsn/fhirportal/dqm/ig/)
* Review the requested FHIR APIs ([QI Core 6 [PDF – 255 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/EHR_QICore6.pdf), [US Core 3 [PDF – 256 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/EHR_USCore3.pdf)) with the EHR vendor for availability and accessibility
* Confirm requested FHIR R4 APIs are in Production and conform to [US Core v6.1.0](https://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/STU6.1/)
* Review [Key Action Items for Reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN [PDF – 86 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/FHIR-Optimizing-Reporting.pdf)
* Review [Overview of Requested FHIR Resources and Standards Adherence for Reporting FHIR dQMs to NHSN [XLS – 26 KB]](/nhsn/xls/dQM-Reporting-Required-FHIR-Data.xlsx)

**IT – Laboratory Data Team**

#### Staff and Facility Roles

**IT – Laboratory Data Team**

* Ensure laboratory test (including central laboratory and point-of-care) types and results are mapped to [LOINC and SNOMED codes](https://www.healthit.gov/isp/sites/isp/files/inline-files/2019ISAReferenceEdition.pdf), respectively
* Ensure [use of best practices](https://loinc.org/kb/faq/mapping/#:~:text=When%20should%20I%20choose%20the,fluid%2C%20not%20serum%20or%20plasma.) when assigning LOINC and SNOMED codes
* Ensure the facility has a plan in place to update LOINC and SNOMED codes in the EHR/FHIR server at least annually

#### Examples of Responsibilities

#### Examples of Responsibilities

* Ensure laboratory test (including central laboratory and point-of-care) types and results are mapped to [LOINC and SNOMED codes](https://www.healthit.gov/isp/sites/isp/files/inline-files/2019ISAReferenceEdition.pdf), respectively
* Ensure [use of best practices](https://loinc.org/kb/faq/mapping/#:~:text=When%20should%20I%20choose%20the,fluid%2C%20not%20serum%20or%20plasma.) when assigning LOINC and SNOMED codes
* Ensure the facility has a plan in place to update LOINC and SNOMED codes in the EHR/FHIR server at least annually

**IT – Medication Data Team**

#### Staff and Facility Roles

**IT – Medication Data Team**

* Ensure medication descriptions in the “Medication” FHIR resource are mapped to RxNorm codes
* Ensure routes of medication administration in the “MedicationAdministration” and “MedicationRequest” resources are mapped to SNOMED codes includes data from third-party pharmacy vendors
* Ensure the facility has a plan in place to update RxNorm and SNOMED codes in the EHR/FHIR server annually, at a minimum

NHSN Digital Quality Measure (dQM) FAQs | NHSN | CDC

# NHSN Digital Quality Measure (dQM) FAQs

## Frequently Asked Questions

NHSNLink

**What is NHSNLink?**

NHSNLink is an open-source HL7 FHIR application for public health reporting. Through an extensible, configurable query engine, NHSNLink connects securely to a healthcare facility’s EHR via the FHIR API  NHSNLink identifies patients of interest from facilities utilizing FHIR standards and passes the data to NHSN for data quality and analytic applications. You can find additional information at [about NHSNLink and FHIR](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html#anchor_96959).

**Does NHSNLink require installation of an external vendor or “third-party” software?**

No, you do not need to install or purchase new software. NHSNLink’s architecture minimizes Information Technology requirements for the participating facility.

**What data security and privacy procedures are in place for data exchange that involves FHIR and PHI/PII with CDC NHSN?**
title: NHSN Digital Quality Measure (dQM) FAQs

ig: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
igTitle: CDC National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) Digital Quality Measures (dQM) Content Package IG
text:
Resource Center Page: NHSN Digital Quality Measure (dQM) FAQs
Page key: faq
URL: https://www.cdc.gov/nhsn/fhirportal/faqs.html
Category: overview
Audience: ehr-vendor-team, laboratory-data-team

inimizes Information Technology requirements for the participating facility.

**What data security and privacy procedures are in place for data exchange that involves FHIR and PHI/PII with CDC NHSN?**

NHSN has over 20 years of experience in secure and confidential data exchange with over 37,000 U.S. hospitals and other healthcare facilities. Data access and protection provisions are outlined in the *NHSN Agreement to Participate and Consent*and the *NHSN Facility/Group User & Administrator Rules of Behavior.*Data are protected by administrative, technical, and physical security controls that safeguard the confidentiality, integrity, and privacy of personal information according to industry-standard policies and federal laws, including the [Privacy Act](https://www.hhs.gov/foia/privacy/index.html), the [Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act](https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/index.html) (HIPAA), and the [Federal Information Security Modernization Act](https://www.cisa.gov/federal-information-security-modernization-act) (FISMA).

Additionally, NHSNLink safeguards information in the following ways:

* Protected by an OAuth2.0 identity-access management (IAM) service delivered by CDC Secure Access Management Services (SAMS), which includes authentication and role-based access control (RBAC).
* Queries only selected, pre-specified FHIR resources that are available only upon authorization from the facility’s server. Access control is provided on a FHIR resource-by-resource basis.
* Data (including internal NHSNLink app communications) are encrypted in-transit through HTTPS/TLS1.2.
* Data are encrypted at-rest using NIST Advanced Encryption Standard 256 (AES 256)
title: NHSN Digital Quality Measure (dQM) FAQs

esource basis.
* Data (including internal NHSNLink app communications) are encrypted in-transit through HTTPS/TLS1.2.
* Data are encrypted at-rest using NIST Advanced Encryption Standard 256 (AES 256)

For information about how Protected Health Information (PHI) and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) is secured within NHSNLink, please visit the [security considerations section](/nhsn/fhirportal/about.html#anchor_72781).

**How does NHSNLink differ from Electronic Case Reporting (eCR)?**

Both NHSNLink and [Electronic Case Reporting](/ecr/index.html) (eCR) support CDC’s [Data Modernization Initiative](/surveillance/data-modernization/index.html) by improving data sharing between CDC and its partners, but NHSNLink and eCR have different purposes and serve different public health surveillance needs. NHSNLink support NHSN’s model of national-level surveillance of healthcare events to support quality improvement and benchmarking. eCR supports a model for automated case reporting of reportable public health conditions (e.g., state-reported COVID-19 cases).

Reporting Data

**Will existing non-dQM measures that NHSN supports be transitioned to dQMs?**

Current data-reporting methods will remain in place at this time for existing non-dQMs.

**How do I know that my facility’s data are being submitted correctly and all required fields are populated?**

There is a screening process that will stop the data from being submitted if required fields are not properly populated. These issues must be resolved before the data can be resubmitted.

Data Analysis

**Will the new dQMs have analysis and report options that facilities can use to modify and analyze data, like the other measures featured on NHSN?**
title: NHSN Digital Quality Measure (dQM) FAQs

text:

Page key: faq

Category: overview
Audience: ehr-vendor-team, laboratory-data-team

before the data can be resubmitted.

Data Analysis

**Will the new dQMs have analysis and report options that facilities can use to modify and analyze data, like the other measures featured on NHSN?**

Yes. dQMs have modifiable analysis, and reports are in the NHSN Reports tab.

**How can a facility view line-level data submitted via FHIR****?**

You can view line-level data on the NHSN Reports tab within the application. You can find measure-specific line-list functionality and features in the [analysis resources line list document [PDF – 169 KB]](/nhsn/pdfs/ps-analysis-resources/linelists.pdf).

NHSNLink requires that:

* Your facility has FHIR APIs that are version R4 or higher
* Your facility FHIR vR4 APIs are deployed to your Production environment
* Your facility FHIR vR4 APIs conform to the [US Core Data for Interoperability](https://www.healthit.gov/isa/united-states-core-data-interoperability-uscdi)
* Your facility is familiar with your EHR vendor policies for access to your FHIR R4 APIs by third-party applications for public health and regulatory reporting

You can work with your Information Technology and EHR vendor representative to learn more about whether your facility meets these requirements. Additional information about technical specifications will be released soon.
